mainmatter.p: Let \chi _x \colon a = x be the witness of contraction for any x:A. Given b : Ba and x:A, we define \mathsf {ind}_{ A,a } \, b \, x : Bx by transporting b along the identification \chi _a ^{-1} \cdot \chi _x \colon a = x:
Then we compute \mathsf {ind}_{ A,a } \, b \, x \equiv \mathopen {} \left ( \chi _a ^{-1} \cdot \chi _a \right ) \mathclose {} _* b = \mathsf {refl} _*b = b.

Let A be a contractible type with centre a. For any family of types B \colon A \to \mathsf {Type}, we may define a function \mathsf {ind}_{ A,a } \colon B a \to \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x : A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x : A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x : A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x : A \right ) \mathclose {} } } Bx satisfying \mathsf {ind}_{ A,a } \, b \, a = b.
